name: From Eduardo VII Park to Serra de Monsanto — route in Lisbon
name_original: Do Parque Eduardo VII à Serra de Monsanto — percurso em Lisboa
region_intro: Jogging routes around Coração De Jesus offer a diverse range of experiences, from urban paths to more challenging trails. The region's landscape, as indicated by the available routes, includes varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels. These routes provide opportunities to explore both natural and urban environments. The area features a mix of moderate trails, with some easier and more difficult options available. Best jogging routes around Coração De Jesus The most popular jogging route is From Eduardo VII Park to Serra de Monsanto — route in Lisbon, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This moderate route features a mix of urban parkland and natural areas. Another top favourite among local runners is Small Lake – View from the Rua Augusta Arch loop from Marquês de Pombal, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.4 km) path. This route offers a blend of city views and scenic lake surroundings. Local runners also love the View from the Rua Augusta Arch – Along the Ribeira das Naus loop from Marquês de Pombal, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through urban landscapes and riverside areas, often completed in about 1 hour. Jogging around Coração De Jesus is defined by urban parks, riverside paths, and varied elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails. The routes in Coração De Jesus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 3000 runners have used komoot to explore Coração De Jesus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Coração De Jesus?
A: There are over 15 running routes around Coração De Jesus, offering a diverse range of experiences for different fitness levels. These include easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Coração De Jesus?
A: The jogging routes in Coração De Jesus feature varied terrain. Depending on the location, you might encounter urban parkland, riverside paths, coastal trails, or more challenging hilly sections. The region encompasses diverse ecosystems, from the cerrado and caatinga in Brazil to the mountainous and coastal landscapes of Portugal, providing a mix of natural and urban environments.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Coração De Jesus?
A: Yes, Coração De Jesus offers at least one easy running route, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed jog. Additionally, many of the moderate routes can be adapted for a gentler pace, especially those through urban parks or along flat riverside paths.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging trails in Coração De Jesus?
A: The running routes in Coração De Jesus are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 60 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, scenic views, and the blend of urban and natural landscapes available.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Coração De Jesus?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Coração De Jesus are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32305268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Lake – View from the Rua Augusta Arch loop from Marquês de Pombal&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32215588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from the Rua Augusta Arch – Along the Ribeira das Naus loop from Marquês de Pombal&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in Coração De Jesus?
A: For those seeking longer runs, Coração De Jesus has several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18972582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Discoveries – 25th of April Bridge loop from Marquês de Pombal&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 16.3 km (10.1 miles) route, offering a substantial distance for endurance training.

Q: Are there scenic running trails with viewpoints in Coração De Jesus?
A: Absolutely! If you're near Viana do Castelo, Portugal, the area around the Santa Luzia Sanctuary offers stunning 360-degree panoramic views of the city, the Lima River, and the Atlantic Ocean. In the Brazilian region, you can find scenic spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8178196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eduardo VII Park Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, providing inspiring backdrops for your run.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while jogging in Coração De Jesus?
A: While running in Coração De Jesus, you can discover various natural attractions. In Brazil, the serene Lagoa do Renovação offers a picturesque setting, and some routes might pass by waterfalls like those at Usina and Faz Gameleira. In Portugal, urban green spaces like Parque Ecológico Urbano and coastal paths along beaches like Praia Norte provide beautiful natural scenery. You can also explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5883754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of Parque da Paz&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amália Rodrigues Garden Lake&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the running paths?
A: Yes, Coração De Jesus is rich in history and culture. In Brazil, you might encounter historical buildings like Casa Araújo and Capelinha dos Lafetás. In Portugal, the iconic Santa Luzia Sanctuary with its Byzantine architecture is a prominent landmark. Other notable points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/399846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Overseas Combatants&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Christ the King&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there options for urban jogging or routes through parks in Coração De Jesus?
A: Yes, Coração De Jesus offers excellent options for urban jogging and routes through green spaces. In Brazil, the tranquil streets of the city itself provide a peaceful environment. In Portugal, Viana do Castelo features urban parks like Parque Ecológico Urbano and Parque da Cidade, as well as the well-known &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gulbenkian Garden&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7493881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinta das Conchas and Lilases Park&lt;/a&gt;, which are perfect for a run amidst lush greenery.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Coração De Jesus does offer more difficult routes. The area around Monte de Santa Luzia in Portugal, for instance, features trails with significant elevation changes, including a monumental staircase leading to the sanctuary, providing an invigorating workout. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18972582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Discoveries – 25th of April Bridge loop from Marquês de Pombal&lt;/a&gt; is also classified as a difficult route.

Q: Can I find less crowded running routes in Coração De Jesus?
A: While popular routes can see more traffic, exploring some of the less-trafficked trails or visiting during off-peak hours can help you find quieter running experiences. The diverse network of routes means there are always options to discover a more secluded path, especially in the natural areas away from the main urban centers.

Q: Are there any routes that combine city views with natural surroundings?
A: Many routes in Coração De Jesus beautifully blend urban landscapes with natural elements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381167306/from-eduardo-vii-park-to-serra-de-monsanto-route-in-lisbon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Eduardo VII Park to Serra de Monsanto — route in Lisbon&lt;/a&gt; offers a mix of urban parkland and natural areas, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32305268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Lake – View from the Rua Augusta Arch loop from Marquês de Pombal&lt;/a&gt; provides a blend of city views and scenic lake surroundings.
tips: In the center of the square stands a monument to the Marquis of Pombal, one of the key figures in the history of Lisbon and all of Portugal. On the monument you can see a large figure of the marquis who was the governor of Lisbon between 1750 and 1777, along with a lion, the classic symbol of power.

The Arial is huge and totally impressive. It's hard to resist a certain magic. No wonder that festivals etc. take place here. are held frequently. The view of the city, which you ideally have “hiked” beforehand, is also magically beautiful.

Parque Eduardo VII
This famous park is named after British King Edward VII who visited the city in the twentieth century. Parque Eduardo VII is the largest park in the center of Lisbon. The park is built against a hill.
This point in Monsanto Park offers an unpretentious view of Lisbon. It becomes convenient as a stopping point in the middle of the race.

View of the Águas Livres Aqueduct and the city of Lisbon
